chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content:
Advanced Networking and Security. (3-0). Credit 3. Security aspects of various
network protocols including investigation and tool development using "live"
machines and networks. Prerequisites: Graduate classification and approval of
instructor.

chunks.title: Additional Course Details
chunks.content:
This course will introduce various modern topics in computer and network security. It will provide a
thorough grounding in computer and network security suitable for those interested in conducting research
in this area, as well as students more broadly interested in real-world security issues/techniques. Topics
may span (but not limited to):
• Basic network & system security: e.g., software security, malware, botnet, intrusion detection
• Security in a software-defined world: e.g., SDN/NFV, Cloud, 5G/Edge, Vehicle security
• Mobile & IoT security: e.g., smartphone security, Alexa and voice app security
• Web and Web3 security: e.g., traditional web security, spam, Web3 (DeFi, blockchain) security
• AI/LLM security: e.g., LLM-based security, adversarial Learning / backdoor, advanced applications
with Al

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Graduate classification and approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
• Analyze the complex trade-offs and architectural tensions inherent in securing modern distributed
systems and networks.
• Synthesize fundamental security principles and mechanisms to design robust defenses against
sophisticated threat models.
• Critique state-of-the-art security literature to identify open problems, methodological flaws, and
emerging trends in the field.
• Demonstrate a practical working knowledge of offensive and/or defensive techniques to
contextualize theoretical concepts.

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Recommended
Various papers
Notes:
There is no required textbook. Most readings will be from research papers in top security conferences and
journals (listed below).

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
• Paper mini-review: 25% (No Al tools such as ChatGPT are allowed to use in the review writing)
• Class participation and presentation: 10%
• Mini research project: 65%
There is no mid-term or final exam.
There will be bonus points for EXCELLENT mini-research projects.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not
considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
All late submissions will automatically lose 20% points per delayed day. Submissions five days after the
deadline will NOT be accepted (unless you get permission from the instructor).

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
Tentative Schedule
| Week | Topic |
|---|---|
| 1 | Course logistics & security overview |
| 2 | Malware Basics |
| 3 | Intrusion Detection |
| 4 | Intrusion Detection II |
| 5 | Advanced Detection I |
| 6 | Advanced Detection & Response II |
| 7 | Software-Defined Security |
| 8 | Web/Web3 Security |
| 9 | Spring break |
| 10 | Web/Web3 Security II |
| 11 | AI/LLM Security |
| 12 | AI/LLM Security II |
| 13 | AI/LLM Security III |
| 14 | Reading day |
| 15 | AI/LLM Security IV |
| 16 | Mini-workshop: student project presentation |

chunks.title: Al Statement
chunks.content: No Al tools such as ChatGPT are allowed to use in the review writing.
